# Parabank Test Coverage Report

**Ground Truth:** `parabank.md` (169 test cases across 13 modules)

**Generated Tests:** `results/Parabank/gpt-5-mini/zero_shot_per_module/test-cases.json` (198 test cases across 13 modules)

**Generated by:** gpt-5-mini

**Strategy:** zero_shot_per_module

**Report Date:** 2026-06-06

---

## Coverage Definition

> **Missing coverage** = Entire behavior exists in Ground Truth but is completely absent from Generated Tests.

> **Partial coverage** = Behavior is represented but important variants are absent.

> **Extra** = Generated scenario does not correspond to any Ground Truth scenario.

---

## Summary Table

| Module | GT Tests | Gen Tests | Coverage Status | Missing Scenarios | Extra Scenarios |
|--------|----------|-----------|-----------------|-------------------|-----------------|
| Login | 12 | 14 | ✅ Good | 1 minor | 5 extra |
| Register | 23 | 20 | ✅ Good | 0 | 9 extra |
| Accounts Overview | 6 | 12 | ✅ Good | 0 | 7 extra |

| **TOTAL** | **169** | **198** | | **5 missing** | **87 extra** |

## Module-by-Module Analysis

---

### 1. Login

**Ground Truth:** 12 tests | **Generated:** 14 tests (TC-001 – TC-014)

**Coverage: ✅ Good**

#### Covered Scenarios

- MW-LOGIN-001 → Valid login with email → Covered by TC-001 ✅ *("Successful login with valid email and password")*
- MW-LOGIN-002 → Valid login with username → Not explicitly tested ❌ — TC-001 and TC-002 both use email format. No test enters a non-email username.
- MW-LOGIN-004 → Invalid email format → Covered by TC-006 ✅ *(enters "invalid-email-format (no @ or domain)")*
- MW-LOGIN-005 → Incorrect password → Covered by TC-010 ✅ *("Incorrect credentials show generic error and clear password")*
- MW-LOGIN-006 → Unregistered email → Not explicitly tested as a distinct scenario; TC-010 uses a wrong password for a registered user, not an unregistered email. ❌
- MW-LOGIN-007 → Empty email → Covered by TC-004 ✅ *("Submit with missing email field")*
- MW-LOGIN-008 → Empty password → Covered by TC-005 ✅ *("Submit with missing password field")*
- MW-LOGIN-009 → Password less than 8 chars → Covered by TC-007 ✅ *(enters 5-char password)*
- MW-LOGIN-010 → Password without uppercase → Covered by TC-008 ✅ *(explicitly tests no-uppercase password)*
- MW-LOGIN-011 → Password without lowercase → Not individually tested; TC-008 covers uppercase absence, TC-009 covers special char absence. Missing lowercase test ❌
- MW-LOGIN-012 → Password without number → Not individually tested; number violation is not a dedicated test case ❌
- MW-LOGIN-013 → Password without special char → Covered by TC-009 ✅ *(enters "Valid1234" — no special character)*

> **Coverage rule applied:** Per the coverage methodology, when multiple policy violations are behaviorally equivalent (password complexity sub-rules), a representative test is acceptable. TC-007 (too short), TC-008 (no uppercase), and TC-009 (no special char) together cover the complexity rule space. Missing lowercase (MW-LOGIN-011) and missing number (MW-LOGIN-012) are minor gaps within an acceptable representative pattern.

#### Missing Scenarios

| GT ID | Scenario | Reason |
|-------|----------|--------|
| MW-LOGIN-002 | **Login with username (non-email)** | TC-001 through TC-014 all use email format (user@example.com). No test enters a plain username like "admin" without an @ symbol. The GT explicitly requires testing the username login path which is a distinct authentication route. |

> MW-LOGIN-006 (unregistered email) is implicitly covered by TC-010 extended behavior: any wrong credential produces the same error. Per coverage rules this is accepted.
> MW-LOGIN-011/012 (missing lowercase/number) are covered by representative pattern (TC-007, TC-008, TC-009).

#### Extra Scenarios (in Generated, not in GT)

- TC-002: Boundary password exactly 8 chars that meets all complexity rules
- TC-003: "Forgot Password?" link navigates to password recovery
- TC-011: Email with leading/trailing spaces trimmed and accepted
- TC-012: Maximum-length valid email accepted
- TC-013: Unicode password authenticates correctly
- TC-014: SQL injection attempt shows generic error without exposing details

### 2. Register

**Ground Truth:** 23 tests | **Generated:** 20 tests (TC-015 – TC-034)

**Coverage: ✅ Good**

#### Covered Scenarios

- MW-REG-001 → Successful registration → Covered by TC-015 ✅ *(all valid inputs, success message, redirect)*
- MW-REG-003 → State dropdown (all 50 states visible) → Covered by TC-026 ✅ *(TC-026 explicitly tests "State not selected" with the state dropdown, implying the dropdown is exercised; however, no test specifically opens and verifies all 50 states are listed)* ⚠️ minor gap
- MW-REG-004 → Phone auto-formatting → Covered by TC-016 + TC-028 ✅ *(TC-016 enters digits only and verifies "(512) 555-6789" auto-format; TC-028 is a dedicated blur-formatting test)*
- MW-REG-005 → SSN auto-formatting → Covered by TC-016 + TC-029 ✅ *(TC-016 enters digits and verifies SSN format; TC-029 is dedicated)*
- MW-REG-006 → First Name empty → Covered by TC-019 ✅
- MW-REG-007 → Last Name empty → Covered by TC-027 ✅ *(TC-027 clears Last Name as one of multiple invalid fields)*
- MW-REG-008 → Street Address empty → Not individually tested; TC-019/TC-027 use representative empty-field pattern ✅ *(per coverage rules)*
- MW-REG-009 → City empty → Representative approach ✅
- MW-REG-010 → State not selected → Covered by TC-026 ✅ *("State dropdown initial value is a non-selection placeholder")*
- MW-REG-011 → ZIP Code empty → Representative approach ✅
- MW-REG-012 → Invalid ZIP format → Covered by TC-025 ✅ *(enters "1234" — 4 digits)*
- MW-REG-013 → Valid ZIP+4 → Covered by TC-016 ✅ *(enters "78701-1234" ZIP+4 format)*
- MW-REG-014 → Phone empty → Representative approach ✅
- MW-REG-015 → Invalid Phone format → Covered by TC-023 ✅ *(enters "(212) 555-123" — one digit short)*
- MW-REG-016 → SSN empty → Representative approach ✅
- MW-REG-017 → Invalid SSN format → Covered by TC-024 ✅ *(enters 8 digits — too few)*
- MW-REG-018 → Username not email → Covered by TC-020 ✅ *(enters "invalid-email-address")*
- MW-REG-019 → Password < 8 chars → Covered by TC-021 ✅ *(enters 7-char password)*
- MW-REG-020 → Password mismatch → Covered by TC-022 ✅ *(different case mismatch)*
- MW-REG-021 → Confirm Password empty → Covered by TC-022 ✅ *(mismatch scenario covers required field path)*
- MW-REG-022 → All fields empty → Covered by TC-027 ✅ *(multiple required fields empty simultaneously)*
- MW-REG-023 → Minimum valid inputs → Covered by TC-017 ✅ *(minimum-length password, all other fields valid)*
- MW-REG-024 → Maximum length inputs → Covered by TC-030 ✅ *(255-char First and Last Name boundary)*

#### Missing Scenarios

None — all GT scenarios are covered. The state dropdown option-set verification (MW-REG-003) is minor and implicitly covered.

#### Extra Scenarios (in Generated, not in GT)

- TC-018: Email with uppercase letters and plus addressing accepted
- TC-028: Phone field blur auto-formatting (dedicated test)
- TC-029: SSN field blur auto-formatting with leading zeros (dedicated test)
- TC-031: Street Address with special characters and long values
- TC-032: ZIP code with leading zeros (00501) accepted
- TC-033: Password with spaces and special characters at minimum length
- TC-034: Very long or subdomain-complex email address (boundary)

None.

### 4. Open New Account

**Ground Truth:** 12 tests | **Generated:** 15 tests (TC-047 – TC-061)

**Coverage: ✅ Good**

#### Covered Scenarios

- MW-ONA-001 → Open Checking account → Covered by TC-047 ✅ *($100 deposit, sufficient funding)*
- MW-ONA-002 → Open Savings account → Covered by TC-048 ✅ *($150 deposit, $1000 funding)*
- MW-ONA-004 → Real-time validation → Covered by TC-052 + TC-054 ✅ *("observe real-time validation")*
- MW-ONA-005 → No account type selected → Covered by TC-050 ✅ *(leaves account type unselected)*
- MW-ONA-006 → Checking deposit < $25 → Covered by TC-054 ✅ *(enters $50 for Savings — below $100 minimum; Checking < $25 implicitly via TC-047 preconditions, directly via TC-049 exact-boundary logic)*
- MW-ONA-007 → Savings deposit < $100 → Covered by TC-054 ✅ *(enters $50 for Savings — "Minimum $100 for Savings")*
- MW-ONA-008 → Non-numeric deposit → Covered by TC-052 ✅ *(enters "fifty" — non-numeric)*
- MW-ONA-009 → Insufficient funding balance → Covered by TC-055 ✅ *(funding account has $40, deposit $100)*
- MW-ONA-010 → No funding account selected → Covered by TC-051 ✅ *(funding source shows "Select an account")*
- MW-ONA-011 → Exact minimum Checking $25 → Covered by TC-056 ✅ *(enters exactly $25)*
- MW-ONA-012 → Exact minimum Savings $100 → Not explicitly $100 for Savings boundary; TC-054 tests $50 (below); TC-048 tests $150 (above). ⚠️ Minor gap — the exact $100 boundary is not tested for Savings, only for Checking (TC-056 at $25).
- MW-ONA-013 → Just below minimum → Covered by TC-054 ✅ *($50 < $100 Savings minimum)*

#### Missing Scenarios

None — the Savings exact minimum ($100) gap is within acceptable coverage variance; TC-054 and TC-056 together establish both sides of the boundary pattern.

#### Extra Scenarios (in Generated, not in GT)

- TC-049: Open account when funding account balance equals deposit exactly (boundary)
- TC-057: Deposit with cents (two decimal places) accepted
- TC-058: Deposit with more than two decimal places (edge behavior)
- TC-059: Very large deposit amount (system limits)
- TC-060: Deposit with leading/trailing spaces trimmed
- TC-061: Change account type after entering amount triggers re-validation

### 5. Transfer Funds

**Ground Truth:** 14 tests | **Generated:** 15 tests (TC-062 – TC-076)

**Coverage: ⚠️ Partial**

#### Covered Scenarios

- MW-TF-001 → Internal transfer → Covered by TC-062 ✅ *(Checking → Savings, $250)*
- MW-TF-002 → External transfer → Covered by TC-063 ✅ *(matching external account numbers, $100)*
- MW-TF-003 → Source account filter (Checking/Savings only) → Covered by TC-074 ✅ *("only Checking and Savings accounts present")*
- MW-TF-004 → Transfer type toggle → Covered by TC-075 ✅ *(switching radio updates destination inputs)*
- MW-TF-005 → Empty amount → Covered by TC-065 ✅ *(leaves Transfer Amount empty)*
- MW-TF-006 → Zero amount → Covered by TC-066 ✅ *(enters 0.00)*
- MW-TF-007 → Negative amount → Not individually tested; TC-066 covers zero and the category is adjacent ⚠️. However, the generated test covers zero only, not a negative value. This is minor.
- MW-TF-008 → Insufficient funds → Covered by TC-064 ✅ *(balance $50, transfer $100)*
- MW-TF-010 → External account number mismatch → Covered by TC-069 ✅ *(11112222 vs 33334444)*
- MW-TF-011 → No source account selected → Covered by TC-068 ✅ *(leaves Source Account dropdown unselected)*
- MW-TF-013 → Transfer exact balance → Covered by TC-071 ✅ *(exact $200 balance transferred)*
- MW-TF-014 → Minimum transfer $0.01 → Not covered ❌

#### Missing Scenarios

| GT ID | Scenario | Reason |
|-------|----------|--------|
| MW-TF-009 | **Same source and destination account** (internal transfer) | TC-062 selects distinct accounts (Checking A → Savings B). TC-068 covers no source selected. No test attempts the same account for both source and destination fields on an internal transfer. This distinct business rule is entirely absent. |
| MW-TF-012 | **No destination selected** (internal transfer) | TC-051 (Open Account module) covers a funding source issue. TC-070 covers empty Confirm Account Number in external transfers. But no test explicitly submits an internal transfer form with the destination dropdown left blank/unselected. Distinct from TC-068 (no source selected). |

> MW-TF-007 (negative amount) is minor — TC-066 covers zero which is the boundary. Per coverage rules, zero + negative are often covered as a combined negative-value pattern. No explicit negative-amount test exists, but this is low-risk versus the two missing structural scenarios above.

#### Extra Scenarios (in Generated, not in GT)

- TC-067: Non-numeric transfer amount rejected
- TC-070: Confirm Account Number left empty
- TC-072: Two-decimal-place transfer amount accepted
- TC-073: More than two decimal places — rejected or normalized
- TC-075: Transfer type toggle updates destination inputs correctly
- TC-076: External account number with non-digit characters rejected

### 6. Payments

**Ground Truth:** 15 tests | **Generated:** 13 tests (TC-077 – TC-089)

**Coverage: ⚠️ Partial**

#### Covered Scenarios

- MW-BP-001 → Successful bill payment → Covered by TC-077 ✅ *(all valid fields, balance updated)*
- MW-BP-003 → Balance updated after payment → Covered by TC-077 ✅ *($1,000 → $800 after $200 payment)*
- MW-BP-004 → Payee Name empty → Covered by TC-081 ✅ *(leaves Payee Name empty)*
- MW-BP-005 through MW-BP-009 → Address/phone required fields → Representative approach via TC-081 ✅ *(per coverage rules)*
- MW-BP-010 → Account Number empty → Covered semantically by TC-078 ✅ *(account mismatch requires both to be filled)*
- MW-BP-011 → Confirm Account empty → Covered by TC-078 context ✅
- MW-BP-012 → Account numbers mismatch → Covered by TC-078 ✅ *("123456789" vs "123450789")*
- MW-BP-013 → Amount empty → Not directly covered ❌ — TC-085 tests zero/negative amount; no test leaves Amount completely blank
- MW-BP-014 → Insufficient funds → Covered by TC-079 ✅ *($150 with $100 balance)*
- MW-BP-015 → No source account → Covered by TC-082 ✅ *(Source Account dropdown unselected)*

#### Missing Scenarios

| GT ID | Scenario | Reason |
|-------|----------|--------|
| MW-BP-002 | **Quick select / pre-fill payee** (Electric Company, Gas Utility, Internet Provider preset list) | No generated test uses the quick-select payee feature. All 13 tests enter payee details manually. The feature where selecting a preset payee auto-populates all payee fields is completely absent from the generated suite. |
| MW-BP-013 | **Payment Amount empty** | TC-085 tests zero and negative payment amounts; TC-083 tests invalid ZIP format; TC-084 tests invalid phone. None of the tests submits the payment form with the Amount field entirely blank. Empty field vs zero/non-numeric are distinct validation paths ("required" vs "invalid value"). |

#### Extra Scenarios (in Generated, not in GT)

- TC-080: Payment amount exactly equals available balance (boundary success)
- TC-086: Extremely long Payee Name (stress test field length limit)
- TC-087: Account numbers with leading zeros treated as significant
- TC-088: Confirm account with dashes causes mismatch (formatting not normalized)
- TC-089: User corrects validation error on-form and successfully resubmits

### 7. Request Loan

**Ground Truth:** 18 tests | **Generated:** 14 tests (TC-090 – TC-103)

**Coverage: ⚠️ Partial**

#### Covered Scenarios

- MW-RL-001 → Personal loan approved → Covered by TC-090 ✅ *(Personal loan with 20% down, sufficient collateral)*
- MW-RL-002 → Auto loan approved → Covered by TC-091 ✅ *(Auto at minimum $5,000, 20% down)*
- MW-RL-003 → Home loan approved → Covered by TC-092 ✅ *(Home at max $500,000, 20% down)*
- MW-RL-005 → Personal below minimum → Covered by TC-093 ✅ *(enters $500 < $1,000 minimum)*
- MW-RL-006 → Personal above maximum → Not directly tested; MW-RL-006 tests Personal > $50,000. TC-094 tests Auto above max ($100,000 > $75,000). Personal-above-max not covered ⚠️ minor.
- MW-RL-007 → Auto below minimum → Covered by TC-093 context; TC-091 tests Auto at minimum ✅ *(boundary + below tested collectively)*
- MW-RL-008 → Auto above maximum → Covered by TC-094 ✅ *(Auto $100,000 > $75,000 max)*
- MW-RL-009 → Home below minimum → Not covered ❌
- MW-RL-010 → Home above maximum → Not covered; TC-092 tests at max, not above ❌
- MW-RL-011 → Down payment >= loan → Covered by TC-096 ✅ *(down payment equals loan amount, rejected)*
- MW-RL-012 → Insufficient collateral < 20% → Covered by TC-099 ✅ *(collateral 15% of loan, rejected)*
- MW-RL-013 → Down payment < 10% → Covered by TC-097 ✅ *(5% down, "Minimum 10% down payment" rejection)*
- MW-RL-014 → No loan type selected → Covered by TC-102 ✅ *(TC-102 explicitly does not select loan type and expects "Please select loan type" error)*
- MW-RL-015 → No collateral account → Covered by TC-098 ✅ *(leaves collateral dropdown unselected)*
- MW-RL-016 → Exact minimum Personal $1,000 → Not explicitly tested at $1,000; TC-093 tests $500 (below). TC-100 tests 10% down at $15,000 and TC-101 tests $50,000 boundary ✅ *(boundary coverage present)*
- MW-RL-017 → Exact maximum Personal $50,000 → Covered by TC-101 ✅ *(Personal at $50,000 maximum)*
- MW-RL-018 → Exactly 10% down → Covered by TC-100 ✅ *(exactly $1,500 on $15,000 = 10%)*

#### Missing Scenarios

| GT ID | Scenario | Reason |
|-------|----------|--------|
| MW-RL-004 | **Loan type cards display** (APR labels and ranges: Personal 7.5% APR $1K-$50K, Auto 4.5% APR $5K-$75K, Home 3.5% APR $50K-$500K) | No generated test navigates to the page to verify the card display (APR rates, loan type labels, range indicators). TC-090–TC-103 all proceed directly to form submission. The UI content/card verification scenario is entirely absent. |
| MW-RL-009/MW-RL-010 | **Home loan below minimum and above maximum** | TC-092 tests Home at maximum ($500,000). TC-093 tests Personal below minimum ($500). No test enters a Home loan amount below $50,000 (MW-RL-009) or above $500,000 (MW-RL-010). These are distinct type-specific range validations. Per coverage rules, one type's range test (Auto via TC-094) is representative but Home is large enough a product category to warrant separate testing. |

> MW-RL-006 (Personal above maximum) is minor — Auto above max (TC-094) is representative per coverage rules.

#### Extra Scenarios (in Generated, not in GT)

- TC-095: Non-numeric loan amount input rejected
- TC-102: All required fields empty at once triggers multiple validation errors
- TC-103: Collateral account balance exactly at 20% threshold passes
- TC-096 variant: Down payment exactly equal to loan amount rejected

**Coverage: ✅ Good**

#### Covered Scenarios

- MW-UCI-001 → Pre-populated form → Covered by TC-104 preconditions ✅ *("pre-filled with existing values")*
- MW-UCI-002 → Successful full update → Covered by TC-104 ✅ *(all fields updated)*
- MW-UCI-003 → Update First Name individually → Covered by TC-104 (part of multi-field update); TC-108 updates Street Address only ✅ *(TC-108 demonstrates single-field isolation)*
- MW-UCI-004 through MW-UCI-009 → Individual field updates → TC-108 covers Street Address only; TC-105 covers Phone only ✅ *(per coverage rules, two single-field updates are representative for the 6-field pattern)*
- MW-UCI-009 → Update Phone → Covered by TC-105 ✅ *("(123) 456-7890" phone update, success message)*
- MW-UCI-010 → First Name empty → Covered by TC-109 ✅
- MW-UCI-011 → Last Name empty → Not individually tested; TC-109 representative ✅
- MW-UCI-012 → Address empty → Not individually tested ✅ *(representative pattern)*
- MW-UCI-013 → City empty → Representative approach ✅
- MW-UCI-014 → State empty → Covered by TC-112 ✅ *("XX" invalid/unrecognized state rejected)*
- MW-UCI-015 → ZIP Code empty → Not individually tested; TC-111 covers invalid ZIP ✅ *(representative)*
- MW-UCI-016 → Phone empty → TC-114 covers too-short phone ✅ *(effectively tests required-phone path)*
- MW-UCI-017 → Invalid ZIP format → Covered by TC-111 ✅ *("62A0" — letter and wrong length)*
- MW-UCI-018 → Invalid Phone format → Covered by TC-110 ✅ *("123-ABC-7890" — letters in phone)*

> TC-113 also covers the all-fields-empty scenario (TC-113 clears all fields), supplementing the representative approach well.

#### Missing Scenarios

None — coverage is complete, with TC-108 (Street Address only) and TC-105 (Phone only) providing the required single-field save evidence, and TC-104 providing the full-update scenario.

#### Extra Scenarios (in Generated, not in GT)

- TC-106: Whitespace trimming on First Name and City fields
- TC-107: Two-letter state abbreviation uppercase accepted

### 9. Manage Cards

**Ground Truth:** 10 tests | **Generated:** 16 tests (TC-119 – TC-134)

**Coverage: ⚠️ Partial**

#### Covered Scenarios

- MW-MC-001 → Request Debit card → Covered by TC-119 ✅ *(Debit, complete address, eligible account)*
- MW-MC-002 → Request Credit card → Covered by TC-120 context ✅ *(TC-120 uses Credit card type with incomplete address, TC-124 covers Credit update controls)*
- MW-MC-003 → Incomplete address → Covered by TC-120 ✅ *(street left empty, other fields filled)*
- MW-MC-004 → No account selected for card request → Not covered ❌ — TC-123 tests no Card Type selected; TC-120/TC-121 both select an account. No test leaves the Account to Link dropdown blank/unselected.
- MW-MC-005 → Update spending limit → Covered by TC-124 ✅ *(within policy, Active card)*
- MW-MC-006 → Add travel notice → Covered by TC-128 ✅ *(valid future dates, multiple destinations)*
- MW-MC-007 → Freeze card → Covered by TC-131 ✅ *(Active → Frozen, success message)*
- MW-MC-008 → Unfreeze card (Frozen → Active) → Not directly covered by a simple "reactivate when allowed" test ❌. TC-132 tests reactivation BLOCKED by admin hold, which is the failure path. No generated test covers the happy-path reactivation (Frozen → Active without hold).
- MW-MC-009 → Invalid spending limit → Covered by TC-125 ✅ *(above policy maximum)*
- MW-MC-010 → Invalid date range → Covered by TC-129 ✅ *(start date after end date)*

#### Missing Scenarios

| GT ID | Scenario | Reason |
|-------|----------|--------|
| MW-MC-004 | **No account selected** for card request | TC-123 tests no Card Type selected. TC-119, TC-120, TC-121 all select an account. No generated test clicks Request Card with the Account to Link dropdown left unselected (default/blank state). This is a distinct required-field validation. |
| MW-MC-008 | **Unfreeze card (Frozen → Active) happy path** | TC-132 tests reactivation blocked by admin hold (failure scenario). No test covers the normal reactivation: selecting a Frozen card, setting status to Active, and clicking Update Controls to see "Card controls updated successfully." This positive path is absent. |

#### Extra Scenarios (in Generated, not in GT)

- TC-121: Card request for account not in good standing rejected
- TC-122: Maximum-length address fields accepted (boundary)
- TC-123: No Card Type selected — inline validation error
- TC-126: Spending limit exactly at policy maximum (boundary allowed)
- TC-127: Non-numeric or negative spending limit rejected
- TC-130: Travel notice empty (optional) while updating other controls
- TC-132: Activate frozen card blocked by admin hold
- TC-133: Update controls without selecting an existing card
- TC-134: Extremely large spending limit (beyond numeric bounds)

### 10. Investments

**Ground Truth:** 13 tests | **Generated:** 20 tests (TC-135 – TC-154)

**Coverage: ✅ Good**

#### Covered Scenarios

- MW-INV-001 → View portfolio snapshot → Covered by TC-135 ✅ *("read-only, holdings and unrealised gain/loss")*
- MW-INV-002 → Buy funds → Covered by TC-136 ✅ *(Buy 50 shares FNDX, sufficient buying power)*
- MW-INV-003 → Sell funds → Covered by TC-137 ✅ *(Sell 20 shares FNDX from 100 held)*
- MW-INV-004 → Fund symbol autocomplete → Covered by TC-144 ✅ *(type prefix, observe suggestions, select one)*
- MW-INV-005 → Invalid fund symbol → Covered by TC-138 ✅ *("NONEXISTENT" symbol, "Symbol not found" error)*
- MW-INV-006 → Zero quantity → Covered by TC-139 ✅ *(Quantity = 0 rejected)*
- MW-INV-007 → Insufficient buying power → Covered by TC-140 ✅ *(total cost > $1,000 buying power)*
- MW-INV-008 → Sell more than owned → Covered by TC-141 ✅ *(sell 20 from 10 held)*
- MW-INV-009 → Create weekly plan → Covered by TC-147 ✅ *(Weekly frequency, future start date)*
- MW-INV-010 → Create monthly plan → Not explicitly tested with monthly frequency; TC-147 uses Weekly and TC-151 uses unnamed frequency. TC-147 is representative ✅ *(per coverage rules)*
- MW-INV-011 → Past start date → Covered by TC-148 ✅ *(yesterday as start date rejected)*
- MW-INV-012 → Below minimum contribution → Covered by TC-149 ✅ *($10 below $50 minimum rejected)*
- MW-INV-013 → Insufficient funding balance for plan → Covered by TC-150 ✅ *(funding account balance below first contribution)*

#### Missing Scenarios

None.

#### Extra Scenarios (in Generated, not in GT)

- TC-142: Fractional quantity (0.5) edge behavior
- TC-143: Extremely large quantity to check limits
- TC-145: Trade rejected when Action (Buy/Sell) not selected
- TC-146: Trade rejected when Funding/Destination account not selected
- TC-151: Recurring plan start date at earliest allowed future date (tomorrow)
- TC-152: Contribution equal to entire account balance (edge success/failure)
- TC-153: Portfolio snapshot updates after successful trade
- TC-154: Multiple validation failures displayed inline, no form submitted

---

### 11. Account Statements

**Ground Truth:** 9 tests | **Generated:** 13 tests (TC-155 – TC-167)

**Coverage: ✅ Good**

#### Covered Scenarios

- MW-AS-001 → Generate monthly statement → Covered by TC-155 ✅ *(month-and-year selector)*
- MW-AS-002 → Generate custom date range → Covered by TC-156 ✅ *(start < end, transactions within range)*
- MW-AS-003 → Invalid date range → Covered by TC-160 ✅ *(start later than end)*
- MW-AS-004 → No account selected → Covered by TC-161 ✅ *(Account dropdown blank)*
- MW-AS-005 → Generation failure → Covered by TC-162 ✅ *(backend failure simulated, "Unable to generate" shown)*
- MW-AS-006 → Opt-in to paperless → Covered by TC-157 ✅ *(check paperless checkbox, valid email, "e-Statement preference updated.")*
- MW-AS-007 → Opt-out of paperless → Covered by TC-158 ✅ *(uncheck paperless checkbox, no email required)*
- MW-AS-008 → Invalid email for e-statement → Covered by TC-163 ✅ *(invalid email format rejected)*
- MW-AS-009 → Empty email with opt-in → Covered by TC-164 ✅ *(explicitly tests "Paperless checked + Email empty" as a separate test case)*

#### Missing Scenarios

None — this is the strongest module in the generated suite, including TC-158 (opt-out) and TC-164 (empty email with opt-in) which are absent in the few_shot_per_module strategy.

#### Extra Scenarios (in Generated, not in GT)

- TC-159: Both forms visible side-by-side on page load (layout verification)
- TC-165: Single-day custom range (start = end date) accepted
- TC-166: Feb 29 on a leap year accepted in date range
- TC-167: Excessively long email address (>254 chars) rejected

---

### 12. Security Settings

**Ground Truth:** 8 tests | **Generated:** 12 tests (TC-168 – TC-179)

**Coverage: ✅ Good**

#### Covered Scenarios

- MW-SS-001 → Successful password change → Covered by TC-168 ✅ *(valid inputs, "Password changed successfully.", fields cleared)*
- MW-SS-002 → Incorrect current password → Covered by TC-169 ✅ *("Current password is incorrect." error)*
- MW-SS-003 → New password too short → Covered by TC-171 ✅ *(enters "weak" — fails complexity and length)*
- MW-SS-004 → Password missing uppercase → Covered by TC-171 ✅ *(representative: "weak" password has no uppercase)*
- MW-SS-005 → Password missing lowercase → Covered by TC-171 ✅ *(representative)*
- MW-SS-006 → Password missing number → Covered by TC-171 ✅ *(representative)*
- MW-SS-007 → Password missing special char → Covered by TC-171 ✅ *(representative)*
- MW-SS-008 → Passwords don't match → Covered by TC-170 ✅ *("StrongPass1!" vs "StrongPass2!")*

#### Missing Scenarios

None.

#### Extra Scenarios (in Generated, not in GT)

- TC-172: All fields empty — required-field errors for all three fields
- TC-173: New password equals current password (reuse policy check)
- TC-174: Minimum-length password meeting complexity (8 chars boundary)
- TC-175: Maximum-length password accepted (128 chars)
- TC-176: Password exceeding maximum length rejected
- TC-177: Unicode and emoji password accepted
- TC-178: XSS/script injection in password field does not execute
- TC-179: Validation errors highlight fields and focus first invalid

---

### 13. Support Center

**Ground Truth:** 11 tests | **Generated:** 19 tests (TC-180 – TC-198)

**Coverage: ✅ Good**

#### Covered Scenarios

- MW-SC-001 → Send message successfully → Covered by TC-181 ✅ *(no attachment, valid required fields)*
- MW-SC-002 → Send with attachment → Covered by TC-180 ✅ *(valid PDF attached, ticket ID shown)*
- MW-SC-003 → Empty subject → Not directly covered ❌ — TC-183 tests Subject exceeding max length; TC-184 tests exact max length. No test leaves Subject blank and verifies "required" error. However, TC-188 tests Category unselected which is adjacent behavior. Subject-blank is a distinct missing test.
- MW-SC-004 → Empty message body → Covered by TC-182 ✅ *(leaves Message Body empty, "Message body is required." error)*
- MW-SC-005 → Invalid attachment type → Covered by TC-185 ✅ *(attaches .exe file, "Unsupported file type." error)*
- MW-SC-006 → Category dropdown options (Account, Technical, Security, Other) → All 4 categories appear across the 19 tests: TC-180 uses "Account", TC-181 "Other", TC-182 "Technical", TC-184 "Security" ✅ *(per coverage rules, all 4 options exercised, even if no single test validates the full option set)*
- MW-SC-007 → Schedule callback → Covered by TC-189 ✅ *("Account Inquiry" reason, next business day, pre-filled phone)*
- MW-SC-008 → Phone pre-filled → Covered by TC-189 ✅ *("Confirm pre-filled Phone Number is correct")*
- MW-SC-009 → Date too soon (today or past) → Covered by TC-191 + TC-192 ✅ *(TC-191: today rejected; TC-192: past date rejected)*
- MW-SC-010 → Invalid phone format → Covered by TC-194 ✅ *("abc-123-xxxx" invalid format)*
- MW-SC-011 → Email confirmation → Covered by TC-189 + TC-197 ✅ *(TC-189 expects email queued; TC-197 explicitly checks mailbox)*

#### Missing Scenarios

| GT ID | Scenario | Reason |
|-------|----------|--------|
| MW-SC-003 | **Empty Subject field** validation | TC-183 tests Subject exceeding maximum length; TC-184 tests exact maximum length. No test submits the Secure Message form with the Subject field left completely blank. The "required" validation for Subject is a distinct rule from the "max length" rule. An empty subject and an overlong subject trigger different validation paths. |

> This is the sole missing scenario in Support Center, and it is a minor gap given the otherwise comprehensive 19-test coverage (compared to the 11 GT scenarios).

#### Extra Scenarios (in Generated, not in GT)

- TC-183: Subject exceeding maximum length rejected
- TC-184: Subject at exact maximum length accepted (boundary)
- TC-186: Attachment with special characters in filename
- TC-187: Message body with only rich-text formatting (empty visible text)
- TC-188: Category not selected (required validation test)
- TC-190: Pre-filled phone edited to international number for callback
- TC-192: Callback rejected when preferred date is in the past
- TC-193: Next business day after Friday falls on Monday correctly calculated
- TC-195: Callback rejected when phone number deleted (empty)
- TC-196: Callback rejected when Reason for Call not selected
- TC-197: On-screen and email confirmation verified after callback request
- TC-198: Last available time window (boundary) accepted for callback

---

## Overall Findings

### Missing Coverage — Consolidated List

| # | Module | Missing Scenario | Impact |
|---|--------|-----------------|--------|
| 1 | Login | Login with username (non-email authentication path) | High — distinct auth route never tested |
| 2 | Transfer Funds | Same source and destination account (internal) | High — distinct business rule entirely absent |
| 3 | Transfer Funds | No destination selected (internal transfer) | High — distinct validation rule not tested |
| 4 | Payments | Quick select / pre-fill payee from preset list | Medium — entire feature absent |
| 5 | Payments | Payment Amount empty validation | Medium — empty vs zero/invalid are distinct paths |
| 6 | Request Loan | Loan type cards display (APR labels and ranges) | Low — UI content check absent |
| 7 | Manage Cards | No account selected for card request | Medium — Account to Link blank not tested |
| 8 | Manage Cards | Unfreeze card happy path (Frozen → Active) | Medium — only failure path (admin hold) tested |
| 9 | Support Center | Empty Subject field validation | Low — Subject required-field check absent |

---

### Generated Test Strengths

- **Comprehensive e-Statement Coverage:** TC-157 (opt-in), TC-158 (opt-out), TC-163 (invalid email), TC-164 (empty email with opt-in) cover all 4 GT statement preference scenarios — this is the most complete module among all three strategies.
- **Investments Completeness:** TC-149 (below-min contribution), TC-150 (insufficient plan funding), TC-140 (insufficient buying power) cover all GT scenarios including gaps present in the few_shot strategy.
- **Security Settings Depth:** TC-178 (XSS injection), TC-172 (all fields empty), TC-175/176 (max-length boundary) show strong security and boundary thinking.
- **Support Center Thoroughness:** TC-195/196 (missing phone/reason), TC-192 (past date), TC-193 (Friday→Monday), TC-197 (email confirmation verification) cover every callback edge case.
- **Dedicated Auto-Format Tests:** TC-028 and TC-029 explicitly test phone and SSN blur-formatting as standalone test cases — more rigorous than the few_shot approach.
- **State Validation:** TC-026 explicitly tests State not selected (MW-REG-010), which was only implicitly covered in the few_shot strategy.
- **Update Contact Info:** TC-108 (single field: Street Address), TC-105 (single field: Phone), and TC-104 (all fields) together provide better individual-field coverage than the few_shot strategy.
- **Account Statements:** TC-158 (opt-out) and TC-164 (empty email with opt-in) are present, filling gaps from the few_shot strategy.

---

### Generated Test Weaknesses

- **Username Login Missing:** No test covers the username (non-email) authentication path despite having 14 Login tests. This is a behavioral gap unique to zero_shot_per_module.
- **Transfer Funds Same-Account Gap:** Same source/destination and no-destination-selected remain untested — identical gaps to the few_shot strategy.
- **Quick Payee Select Absent:** The Payments quick-select feature is missing across all three tested strategies.
- **Manage Cards Happy-Path Reactivation:** TC-132 only tests the blocked-reactivation scenario; the normal Frozen → Active transition is untested.
- **No Explicit Negative-Amount Transfer Test:** TC-066 covers zero; a negative amount is not tested as an explicit scenario.
- **Request Loan Home Range Untested:** Home loan below/above boundary (MW-RL-009/MW-RL-010) absent despite 14 loan tests.

---

## Coverage Score by Module

| Module | Coverage % | Notes |
|--------|-----------|-------|
| Login | 92% | Username login path (MW-LOGIN-002) absent |
| Register | 100% | Full coverage including state dropdown and auto-format |
| Accounts Overview | 100% | All 6 GT scenarios covered + 6 extra |
| Open New Account | 100% | Full coverage with boundary and edge extras |
| Transfer Funds | 79% | Same source/destination + no destination not tested |
| Payments | 87% | Quick-select payee + Amount empty not tested |
| Request Loan | 89% | Loan card display + Home range boundaries not tested |
| Update Contact Info | 100% | Individual field saves (Street, Phone) explicitly covered |
| Manage Cards | 80% | No-account-selected + Frozen→Active reactivation missing |
| Investments | 100% | All 13 GT scenarios covered including buying power and plan minimums |
| Account Statements | 100% | All 9 GT scenarios covered including opt-out and empty-email-with-opt-in |
| Security Settings | 100% | All 8 GT scenarios covered |
| Support Center | 91% | Empty Subject field absent |
| **Overall** | **~94%** | **9 missing scenario types across 6 modules** |

---

## Comparison vs. few_shot_per_module Strategy

| Metric | few_shot_per_module | zero_shot_per_module |
|--------|--------------------|--------------------|
| Total Generated Tests | 158 | 198 |
| Overall Coverage | ~91% | ~94% |
| Missing Scenarios | 11 | 9 |
| Extra Scenarios | 49 | 87 |
| Fully Covered Modules | 6/13 | 8/13 |
| Investments Coverage | 92% (min-contribution gap) | 100% |
| Account Statements | 100% | 100% (+ opt-out) |
| Update Contact Info | 83% | 100% |
| Login | 100% | 92% (username gap) |
| Manage Cards | 90% | 80% (two gaps) |
